_meta.note: Daemon-backed filesystem WRITE (TC22 A3). This is the action that finally makes [policy.paths] write_allow enforceable. SECURITY: the daemon canonicalizes the PARENT directory (the target file may not exist yet), policy-gates the canonical target deny-first against the default-deny sensitive-suffix list, deny_extra, and write_allow (a miss on a non-empty write_allow is denied as no_allow_rule; an empty list is unconfigured and allows). read_only_observer denies file_write outright; repo_only confines it to $REPO_ROOT. CONSTITUTION V: a file_write audit row is emitted BEFORE the bytes land. The content is bounded (192 KiB, comfortably under MAX_FRAME_BYTES); oversize is rejected with OversizedRequest before any filesystem touch. The write is ATOMIC (temp file in the same dir + rename) so a reader never sees a torn write. RETRY-SAFETY: file_write is classified MUTATING / NON-idempotent in IpcRequest::is_idempotent, so the client self-heal layer never auto-retries a write (no double-write). create_dirs creates missing parents only WITHIN an allowed path (the parent must still pass policy).

invariants: path is required and must be absolute (the daemon has no workspace root)
content is required, UTF-8, and bounded (192 KiB); oversize is rejected with OversizedRequest before any write
create_dirs is optional (default false) and creates parents only within an allowed path; it never widens write_allow
the write is policy-gated by paths.write_allow deny-first in canonical form; a `..` traversal escape is denied
an audit row is emitted BEFORE the write (constitution V); a denied write creates no file
the write is atomic (temp file + rename); a reader never observes a partial write
file_write is MUTATING / non-idempotent and is never auto-retried by the client self-heal layer
